_comment: Fixed eval set for the script stage. Keep these STABLE so scores are comparable across prompt changes. audience/targetSeconds default to kids_3_6 / 60 if omitted. Add/edit briefs freely, but changing the set resets your baseline comparability.

briefs.brief: Pip spots a glittering pebble by the pond; Mo explains why it sparkles; Bo turns PEBBLE into a silly rhyme. They move coop -> pond -> big oak.
briefs.lesson: Looking closely turns a small thing into a big wonder.

briefs.topic: Pip experiences rain for the first time
briefs.brief: Pip has never felt rain. She is unsure, then delighted. Mo shows how plants drink it; Bo makes splashy puddle jokes.
briefs.lesson: New things can be scary at first and wonderful after.

briefs.topic: A mysterious shiny button in the garden
briefs.brief: The chicks find a shiny button and imagine where it came from. Mystery arc: clue -> theory -> truth (it fell off Mrs. Hop's shawl).
briefs.lesson: Asking questions is the fun part of figuring things out.

briefs.topic: Bo tries to carry too many berries
briefs.brief: Bo insists on carrying all the berries alone, makes a mess, pretends it's fine. Pip notices, calls Mo, they fix it together.
briefs.lesson: Asking for help is brave, and things are more fun together.

briefs.topic: Counting the first stars at dusk
briefs.brief: Calm wind-down at the star meadow. The chicks watch the first stars appear and make a gentle bedtime wish.
briefs.lesson: Quiet moments together are their own kind of magic.

briefs.topic: Where does a rainbow come from
briefs.brief: After a rain a rainbow appears. Pip is awestruck; Mo explains sunlight through water drops with a tea-steam comparison; Bo says RAIN-BOW or BRAIN-BOW.
briefs.lesson: Rainbows happen when sunlight passes through water droplets.

briefs.brief: Pip catches her first snowflake and watches it melt. Wonder -> try -> almost-miss -> try-again -> joy as it snows properly.
briefs.lesson: Some beautiful things are gentle and don't last long.

briefs.topic: A strange echo at the big oak
briefs.brief: A sound seems to answer back from the oak's knot-hole. The chicks investigate; it turns out to be Oliver the Owl waking up.
briefs.lesson: A little courage turns a scary mystery into a new friend.
